nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 金伯利 (西澳)
nodes.summary: 金伯利 (西澳)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联15种鸟类，包括尖羽树鸭、北澳玫瑰鹦鹉、Black grasswren等15种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 路德維希·賴興巴赫
nodes.summary: 路德維希·賴興巴赫是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括斑头雁、斑长脚鹬、南极鹱等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 北領地
nodes.summary: 北領地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联25种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、Torresian imperial pigeon、白面鷺等25种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 胡錦鳥
nodes.summary: 胡錦鳥（Gouldian finch，学名 Chloebia gouldiae），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联5个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、气候变化。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: 澳大利亚
nodes.summary: 澳大利亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联221种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、鵲鵝、细斑树鸭等221种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.evidence: |year=2022 |title=Chloebia gouldiae |volume=2022 |article-number=e.T22719744A211561819 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2022-1.RLTS.T22719744A211561819.en |access-date=17 August 2024}} | status2 = EN | status2_system = EPBC | status2_ref = | genus = Chloebia | parent_authority = Reichenbach, 1862 | species = gouldiae | authority = (Gould, 1844) | synonyms_ref = | synonyms = | range_map = Gouldian Finch.png | range_map_caption = Distribution }} The Gouldian finch (Chloebia gouldiae), also known as the Gould's finch or the rainbow finch, is a colourful passerine bird that is native to Australia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Black-headed male Gouldian finch Gouldian finches are native to northern Australia, in particular the Kimberley and Northern Territory. Prior to the Australian government's ban on the export of Australian fauna, Gouldian finches were exported worldwide, which has resulted in viable captive breeding populations being held in many countries

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Black-headed male Gouldian finch Gouldian finches are native to northern Australia, in particular the Kimberley and Northern Territory. Prior to the Australian government's ban on the export of Australian fauna, Gouldian finches were exported worldwide, which has resulted in viable captive breeding populations being held in many countries

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: This species has been considered an Endangered species by the Australian Government in the last two "Endangered Species Act"s - the Australian Endangered Species Protection Act (ESPA) of 1992 and the Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation (EPBC) Act, 1999. Its status on the IUCN Red List is currently Least concern, but it was considered to be endangered previously: Threatened in 1988, EN in six assessments between 1994 and 2008, and Near threatened in three assessments between 2012 and 2016. The number of Gouldian finches in the wild decreased dramatically in the 20th century due to human-caused habitat loss.{{Cite journal |last=Tidemann |first=Sonia C

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: |date=March 1996 |title=Causes of the decline of the Gouldian Finch Erythrura gouldiae |journal=Bird Conservation International |language=en |volume=6 |issue=1 |pages=49–61 |doi=10.1017/S0959270900001301 |issn=|doi-access=free }} The population went from hundreds of thousands in the early 20th century to 2,500 or fewer by the 1980s. The current estimated population continues to be 2,500 or fewer birds. Early research suggested that a parasite called the air sac mite was responsible for the species' decline, but the mite is no longer considered a major factor. The primary threat to wild Gouldian finch populations is an increase in extensive wildfires in the late dry season of its native habitat, which negatively impacts the availability of both tree hollows for breeding, and the seeds that comprise the bulk of the Gouldian finch's diet. Cyclones and climate change have also negatively impacted tree hollow availability in the Northern Territory.{{Cite news |last=Abbatangelo |first=Ben |date=2023-07-11 |title=Binybara is not just home to the Gouldian finch
